turns.content: I've been thinking about how a Rust HashMap works, but I keep getting confused by the fact that when you insert an item it seems to mutate the structure in place. Is there something like this where updating data doesn't actually change the existing nodes?

That sounds very similar to the concept of persistent data structures used in crates like im or tree_set, where updates create a new version of the root without modifying old paths. Instead of mutating a Red-Black Tree with its borrow checker constraints, these libraries use structural sharing so you can have many versions of the same tree simultaneously. The key difference is that when you perform an update operation on the node, it returns a completely fresh tree rather than returning the same reference to the modified original.
